brief_justification: Paper A's machinery is an online-learning recurrent sequence model: fast weights updated by gradient descent at test time (truncated BPTT) that compress a long visuomotor history into weight space for robot control. Paper B's machinery is a static multivariate embedding for visualization: Homogeneity Analysis (HOMALS) places subjects and category levels in Euclidean space, mapped to RGB and reordered by seriation to render a categorical-data heatmap. These are different mechanisms (gradient-descent-driven dynamical adaptation vs. a fixed correspondence-analysis embedding plus reordering); the only overlap is the generic notion of 'representing data in a lower-dimensional space,' which is not shared transferable machinery, so no role-for-role mapping holds.
